metadata.purpose: DB-load staging for M15-A VCG cleanup. Three operations for a future directive: (1) create new VCGs in verse_context_group + vcg_term link rows; assign verses via verse_context.group_id. (2) Apply set-aside reason to verses with no inner-being phenomenon. (3) Re-route 5 verses from M15-A to M15-E by updating verse_context.cluster_subgroup_id (per-verse override since the term has verses in both sub-groups).
metadata.anchor_status: Not designated in input. The cluster instruction §9 requires at least one anchor per VCG. Anchor designation is a follow-up step before this staging can be turned into a directive.

vcg_creations.label: Wisdom as God's own attribute
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names wisdom as belonging to God, to Christ as the embodied wisdom of God, or to Wisdom personified as God's own deliberative inner capacity. Inner-being phenomenon: divine inner attribute. The verses describe wisdom not as something held by humans but as something inhering in God himself — depths of God, attribute of the Lamb, content of doxology, what God possesses in fullness.

verses.assignment_note: Wisdom of God personified as divine deliberative agent (Luk 11:49)
Wisdom as a depth of God (Rom 11:33)
Christ the wisdom of God (1Cor 1:24)
Christ became to us wisdom from God (1Cor 1:30)
Secret and hidden wisdom of God decreed before ages (1Cor 2:7)
God's manifold wisdom made known through the church (Eph 3:10)
All treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden in Christ (Col 2:3)
Wisdom belongs to the Lamb in heavenly doxology (Rev 5:12)
Wisdom belongs to God in heavenly doxology (Rev 7:12)
To the only wise God be glory (Rom 16:27)
God's understanding as the faculty by which he gives wisdom (Dan 2:21, biynah)
Wisdom belongs to God (Dan 2:20, chokhmah)
God excellent in wisdom (Isa 28:29, tushiyyah)
God wise in heart and mighty in strength (Job 9:4)
God is wise — divine attribute (Isa 31:2)
None among the wise of nations is like the Lord (Jer 10:7)
God holds secrets of wisdom, manifold in understanding (Job 11:6, tushiyyah)
With God are strength and sound wisdom (Job 12:16, tushiyyah)
Wisdom personified claims sound wisdom (Pro 8:14)

vcg_creations.label: Wisdom as inner endowment given by God to a named recipient
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names wisdom as a divinely-given inner endowment placed into a specific named person — Jesus growing in it, Solomon receiving it in answer to prayer, Daniel receiving it through the Spirit, the sanctuary craftsmen filled with the Spirit of skill, apostolic believers receiving it through Christ. Inner-being phenomenon: received inner capacity. The common feature is that the verse names the gift, the giver, or the recipient — wisdom comes into the person from God.

Jesus increased in wisdom (Luk 2:52)
Jesus's wisdom astonishes hearers (Mat 13:54)
Wisdom given to Jesus (Mar 6:2)

Seven men full of Spirit and wisdom (Act 6:3)
Stephen's wisdom and Spirit (Act 6:10)

Moses instructed in wisdom of the Egyptians (Act 7:22)

vcg_creations.label: Wisdom as the settled inner character of the wise person
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names wisdom as the standing inner quality of a person — not the moment of receiving, but the constituted character. The wise person walks, hears instruction, restrains impulse, governs speech, builds, perceives the right time, is teachable, is recognised by their fruits. Inner-being phenomenon: constituted character. Wisdom as a settled inner disposition that shapes what the person does across the whole of their life.

vcg_creations.label: The inner mind set on something (orientation of the inner person)
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names what the inner faculty is directed toward — flesh or Spirit, things of God or things of man, things above or things earthly. Inner-being phenomenon: directional orientation of the mind. The verses describe the object on which the inner person is set, not the constitution of their character. The mind has an inner focus, and the verses name what that focus is.

verses.assignment_note: Mind on things of God, not man (Mat 16:23)
Mind on things of God (Mar 8:33)
Minds set on flesh vs Spirit (Rom 8:5)
Observance in honor of the Lord (Rom 14:6)
Think with sober judgment (Rom 12:3)
Set minds on things above (Col 3:2)
Paul holds Philippians in his heart (Phili 1:7)
Have this mind in Christ Jesus (Phili 2:5)
Those mature think this way (Phili 3:15)
Minds set on earthly things (Phili 3:19)
Revived concern for me (Phili 4:10)
We desire to hear your views (Act 28:22)
Confidence you will take no other view (Gal 5:10)

vcg_creations.label: Inner concord between persons (same-mindedness in community)
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names the inner state of unity or harmony between members of a community — being of the same mind, in accord, in agreement. Inner-being phenomenon: shared inner orientation between persons. Distinct from VCG04 in that the phenomenon is concord between people, not the direction of one person's mind. The verses describe inner mutual alignment as a community state.

verses.assignment_note: God grant you to live in harmony (Rom 15:5)
Agree with one another (2Cor 13:11)
Being of the same mind (Phili 2:2)
Agree in the Lord (Phili 4:2)

vcg_creations.label: Wisdom claimed as one's own (wise in own eyes)
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names the inner posture of self-attributed wisdom — wisdom held as personal possession, the inner stance of believing oneself wise. Inner-being phenomenon: self-claim to wisdom. Distinct from VCG03 (settled character) because the verses are not about the constitution of wisdom but about the person's own claim or self-estimation. Distinct from VCG07 because the focus is the individual inner posture, not the worldly category.

verses.assignment_note: Answer fool lest he be wise in own eyes (Pro 26:5)
Wise in his own eyes (Pro 26:12)
Sluggard wiser in own eyes (Pro 26:16)
Rich man wise in own eyes (Pro 28:11)
Be not wise in your own eyes (Pro 3:7)
Woe to those wise in own eyes (Isa 5:21)
Wise in their own conceit (Job 37:24)
Wise in your own sight (Rom 11:25)

vcg_creations.label: Worldly wisdom under judgment
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names the inner orientation that constitutes 'wisdom of this age' or 'wisdom of the world' — overturned by the cross or by divine action. Inner-being phenomenon: the orientation of mind that operates apart from God and is exposed as foolishness. The Pauline cross-wisdom reversal is the centre; OT verses where God renders wise men foolish belong here also. Includes wisdom turned to deceit, cunning, or craftiness directed against truth or against the people of God.

verses.assignment_note: Wisdom of eloquent speech empties cross (1Cor 1:17)
God destroys wisdom of the wise (1Cor 1:19 sofia)
Discernment of discerning God thwarts (1Cor 1:19 sunesis)
Wisdom of the wise destroyed (1Cor 1:19 sofos)
Where is the one who is wise (1Cor 1:20 sofos)
God made foolish wisdom of world (1Cor 1:20)

Greeks seek wisdom (1Cor 1:22)

God chose foolish to shame the wise (1Cor 1:27)

Not plausible words of wisdom (1Cor 2:4)
Faith not in wisdom of men (1Cor 2:5)
Wisdom of this world is folly with God (1Cor 3:19)
Catches wise in craftiness (1Cor 3:19 sofos)
Lord knows thoughts of wise — futile (1Cor 3:20)
Not by earthly wisdom but by grace (2Cor 1:12)

Hidden from the wise (Mat 11:25)

Princes of Zoan claim wise ancestry (Isa 19:11)
God turns wise men back, makes knowledge foolish (Isa 44:25)

vcg_creations.label: The inner faculty of grasping / comprehending
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names the perceptive inner capacity — the act of grasping meaning, understanding what is heard or read, interpreting what is shown. Inner-being phenomenon: inner intelligibility. Distinct from VCG03 because the focus is on the moment or act of comprehending, not the standing character of the person. The verses describe the inner faculty by which sense is made.

verses.assignment_note: People understood the reading (Neh 8:8 bin)
Gave the sense (Neh 8:8 sekhel)
Consider — perceive and respond (Jer 9:17)
Whoever is wise let him understand (Hos 14:9 bin)
Whoever is discerning let him know (Hos 14:9 yada)
Wise understand God's ways (Hos 14:9 chakham)
Wisdom: calculate number of beast (Rev 13:18)
Mind with wisdom interprets vision (Rev 17:9)
God closed their hearts to understanding (Job 17:4)
Who is so wise to understand this (Jer 9:12)

vcg_creations.label: Covenantal wisdom: wisdom rooted in fear of the Lord
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names wisdom as the inner state that arises from, or is constituted by, the fear of the Lord, attentiveness to God's ways, or the practice of God's law. Inner-being phenomenon: covenantal inner orientation. Distinct from VCG03 (general wise character) because the verses explicitly name fear of the Lord or covenant practice as the source or substance of the wisdom.

verses.assignment_note: Wisdom and understanding seen by nations through obedience (Deu 4:6)
Fear of the Lord beginning of wisdom (Psa 111:10)
Sound wisdom to fear God's name (Mic 6:9)

vcg_creations.label: Inner forming faculty for sacred design (Spirit-aided design-devising)
vcg_creations.context_description: The term names the inner act of devising artistic designs for sacred construction — the mental forming of pattern that precedes and directs the physical work. Inner-being phenomenon: inner creative-design capacity for sacred purpose. Restricted to the Exodus verses where 'to devise artistic designs' is named as the activity, in the context of Spirit-given craftsmanship for the tabernacle. Distinct from VCG02 because the focus is on the forming activity rather than the moment of endowment.

verses.assignment_note: Devise artistic designs (Exo 31:4)
Devise artistic designs (Exo 35:32)
Designer's inner capacity (Exo 35:35)
Oholiab the engraver and designer (Exo 38:23)

setasides.context_description: Verses where the M15-A term names a public role (court wise-man class as foil), a craft skill applied externally to material (sanctuary execution-craft where focus is on the worked-into pattern), a class title without inner-being content, or where the text is disputed. The verses remain part of the cluster's verse pool but are not grouped under an M15-A inner-being VCG.

reroutes.context_description: Verses where the term names purposive inner devising in non-craft contexts — God devising means, Haman devising a plot, deceit-devising, instrument-inventing. The inner-being phenomenon is deliberative planning, which matches the M15-E sub-group rather than M15-A. Recommend re-routing.

verses.assignment_note: Haman devised evil plan (Est 8:3)
Haman's devised plan returned (Est 9:25)
Devise words of deceit (Psa 35:20)
God devises means for banished (2Sa 14:14)
Like David invent instruments (Amo 6:5)
